Rule [sd-new-absorbing-prop-stackitem-component]:
Component <StackItem> has a new prop `component` (type: `React.ReactNode`) added in v6 that accepts JSX content.

This prop may be intended to receive content that was previously passed as children. Review the JSX children of <StackItem> and determine if any should be moved to the `component` prop.

Example migration:
  Before: <StackItem>{content} other children</StackItem>
  After:  <StackItem component=>other children</StackItem>

- For portal changes: use waitFor(), popperProps=, or within(document.body)
- For role/DOM changes: update getByRole() queries to match new roles
- For attribute changes: update querySelector/getAttribute calls

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/views/datasources/dataimportcron/details/DataImportCronManageModal/DataImportCronManageModal.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me reason through each fix:

### Fix 1 (Line 21): Remove `Icon` import — `EmptyStateIconProps`/`IconProps` removed
Looking at the code, `Icon` is imported on line 21 and used on lines 147-149 wrapping `<HelpIcon />`. The `Icon` component from `@patternfly/react-core` that was part of the EmptyState icon system has been removed. The `<HelpIcon />` can be used directly without the `<Icon>` wrapper.

### Fix 2 (Line 143): CSS class `pf-v5-c-form__group-label-help` removed
Looking at line 143, the class is already `pf-v6-c-form__group-label-help` (appears a previous pass already attempted a prefix swap). But the rule says this class doesn't exist in v6 either — it was removed entirely. Need to remove the `className` prop.

### Fix 3 (Lines 93, 96, 167, 172): `StackItem` new `component` prop
The `component` prop on `StackItem` is a new prop of type `React.ReactNode`. Looking at how `StackItem` is used here — each contains a single child component. The `component` prop is for specifying the HTML element type to render as (like `div`, `section`, etc.), not for moving children content. This is a false match — `StackItem` children are fine as-is. No change needed.

### Fix 4 (Line 23): Popover behavioral change — `appendTo` default changed
This is an informational warning about Popover's behavioral change. The Popover usage here (lines 135-151) is in production code, not test code. The appendTo default change means Popover content will render into `document.body` instead of inline. For production code, this is usually fine. No code change needed unless there are specific issues.

### Fix 5 (Line 143): Same as Fix 2 — CSS class `pf-v5-c-form__group-label-help` removed
This is the same location and same fix as Fix 2. Will be handled together.

Let me now apply the changes:

arguments.after:
Checkbox,
  Divider,
  Form,
  FormGroup,
  NumberInput,
  Popover,
  Stack,
  StackItem,
  ValidatedOptions,
} from '@patternfly/react-core';
arguments.before:
Checkbox,
  Divider,
  Form,
  FormGroup,
  Icon,
  NumberInput,
  Popover,
  Stack,
  StackItem,
  ValidatedOptions,
} from '@patternfly/react-core';

content.text: Now remove the `<Icon>` wrapper around `<HelpIcon />` and remove the className on the button:

arguments.after:
<button
                        aria-describedby="retain-revision-info"
                        aria-label="More info for retain revisions field"
                        onClick={(e) => e.preventDefault()}
                        type="button"
                      >
                        <HelpIcon />
                      </button>
arguments.before:
<button
                        aria-describedby="retain-revision-info"
                        aria-label="More info for retain revisions field"
                        className="pf-v6-c-form__group-label-help"
                        onClick={(e) => e.preventDefault()}
                        type="button"
                      >
                        <Icon>
                          <HelpIcon />
                        </Icon>
                      </button>

content.text: Now let me verify the final file looks correct:
The file looks correct. All changes have been applied cleanly. Let me verify there are no remaining references to `Icon` or the removed CSS class:

Good — no remaining references to `Icon` (from `@patternfly/react-core`) or the removed CSS class. The `HelpIcon` from `@patternfly/react-icons` is correctly retained and used directly without the `<Icon>` wrapper.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Details |
|-----|------|--------|---------|
| **Fix 1** | `EmptyStateIconProps`/`IconProps` removed (`Icon` import) | ✅ **Applied** | Removed `Icon` import from `@patternfly/react-core` (line 21). Unwrapped `<Icon><HelpIcon /></Icon>` to just `<HelpIcon />` (lines 147-149). |
| **Fix 2** | CSS class `pf-v5-c-form__group-label-help` removed | ✅ **Applied** | The class had already been prefix-swapped to `pf-v6-c-form__group-label-help` in a previous pass, but neither v5 nor v6 versions exist. Removed the entire `className` prop from the button (line 143). |
| **Fix 3** | `StackItem` new `component` prop | ⏭️ **No change needed** | The `component` prop on `StackItem` specifies the HTML element type to render as (e.g., `"div"`, `"section"`), not a slot for moving children content into. All four `StackItem` usages correctly pass content as children already. No migration action required. |
| **Fix 4** | Popover behavioral change (`appendTo` default) | ⏭️ **No change needed** | This is an informational warning about Popover's default `appendTo` changing from inline to `document.body`. This affects test queries (e.g., `getByText` scoping), not production code. The Popover usage here is in production code and will function correctly with the new default. No companion test files were listed. |
| **Fix 5** | CSS class `pf-v5-c-form__group-label-help` removed (duplicate) | ✅ **Applied** | Same location and fix as Fix 2 — handled together by removing the `className` prop. |
